analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet asserts that a final deal will include a clear snapback mechanism for both UN and unilateral sanctions that does not require persuading many countries, including Russia or China. Evaluation: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not invoked; no reference to values, history, or legacy. US2 (admirable society) – Not relevant; no societal or cultural content. US3 (hostile enemies) – No explicit adversary is labeled immoral or threatening; Russia/China are mentioned procedurally, not as enemies. US4 (force for good) – Not present; the tweet focuses on procedural sanctions design, not on promoting human rights or aiding oppressed peoples. US5 (great international ally) – Not present; no emphasis on allies or support to them. US6 (at the forefront) – Partially implied: the US is shaping an international agreement with a robust enforcement mechanism and minimizing dependence on others, suggesting procedural leadership. However, the tweet doesn’t explicitly claim US leadership in a domain, only describes a feature of a deal. This is too indirect to qualify as promoting US6. Conclusion: The tweet does not clearly promote any listed narrative.
